exemplar.title: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Maintains a clear and sustained focus on the prescribed title throughout.
Draws on both required areas of knowledge (mathematics and natural sciences) with recognizable examples.
Demonstrates awareness of contrasting perspectives by acknowledging both benefits and limitations of models.
weaknesses: Analysis often remains at a descriptive or assertive level, with limited probing of how assumptions shape knowledge claims.
Evaluation of counterclaims is superficial and unsupported by concrete evidence or detailed argument.
Connections to knowledgeâframework concepts (methods, assumptions, scope, validity) are implicit rather than explicitly analysed, weakening overall coherence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the essay shows many of the features of a band 5â6 responseâclear focus on the title, relevant examples from mathematics and natural sciences, and some awareness of alternative points of viewâthe depth of analysis is consistently too descriptive and counterclaims are only superficially treated. The implicit rather than explicit use of knowledgeâframework terminology further undermines coherence. These limitations place the performance more appropriately in band 3â4, meriting 4 marks out of 10. This allocation aligns with the official examinerâs total of 4, reflecting a clear response to the title with basic argumentation but lacking the fully developed evaluation and integration of TOK concepts required for higher bands.

annotations.comment: Invoking Korzybski and von Neumann is insightful, but your discussion remains descriptive. Evaluate how von Neumannâs view on approximation challenges the certainty of mathematical proofs compared to empirical scientific models.

The economic models counterclaim is promising but needs concrete grounding. Cite a specific eventâsuch as the 2008 financial crisisâto show how rational-agent assumptions led to flawed policy decisions.

The conclusion effectively reiterates the utility of models across disciplines. To enhance evaluation, propose criteria for when a modelâs simplifications outweigh its usefulness, adding a critical edge to your closing argument.

Your mention of cultural relativity opens an important TOK perspective. Develop this by giving an example where a model from one cultural context failed in another, highlighting how perspective shapes model utility.

The articulation of the paradoxâthat models simplify concepts to enhance understandingâis concise and demonstrates clear engagement with the prescribed title. This establishes a solid foundation for your discussion.

Introducing Einsteinâs quote adds depth. To strengthen, tie this explicitly to a physics exampleâsuch as perturbation theory in quantum mechanicsâto illustrate the balance between accuracy and practical applicability.

The introduction repeats the prescribed title but could more sharply define the terms âmodelâ and âusefulâ to frame the subsequent analysis. Establishing precise operational definitions early would guide the reader through your argument more effectively.

The examples of calculus and Newtonian mechanics illustrate simplification well. To deepen your analysis, discuss how calculus formally transitions to discrete models (e.g., use of $$\sum$$ versus $$\int$$) and the implications for real-world applications.
The transition to Korzybskiâs map-territory analogy is smooth and maintains narrative coherence, effectively connecting earlier points to a broader conceptual framework.

While the student defines âmodelâ and cites Bartleby, the informal parenthetical reference lacks TOK citation rigor. Adopt a consistent academic citation style (e.g., author-date) and link the definition directly to your own analysis.

Your thesis outlines the areas of knowledge to be discussed but is overly verbose. Consider refining it to specify which criteria (e.g., assumptions or scope) you will use to evaluate each example, improving clarity and focus.

The synthesis of perspectives on model utility is solid. To elevate the analysis, include a concrete case where overreliance on a flawed model produced significant errorâsuch as early climate projectionsâto substantiate your counterclaim.

You discuss perception and intuition in model acceptance but stop short of naming knowledge-framework terms. Explicitly reference âmethods,â âassumptions,â and âscopeâ when analysing examples to strengthen TOK connections.

The section on uncertainty in abstraction could be enriched by referencing a real studyâsuch as chaotic weather systemsâto illustrate how small changes in initial conditions undermine long-term predictability.
The distinction you draw between faith in a modelâs applicability and formal logical consistency demonstrates nuanced understanding of how mathematical certainty differs from empirical support.

The Bohr model example aptly shows model evolution. Enhance the analysis by comparing Bohrâs spectral predictions with experimental data and modern quantum calculations to highlight its limited accuracy.

Your discussion of climate models is relevant but remains general. Delve into specific limitationsâsuch as cloud parametrization or initial-condition sensitivityâto show why simplifications can lead to misleading projections.

To broaden your perspective, contrast Western scientific models with alternative cultural approachesâsuch as Indigenous environmental knowledgeâto illustrate how cultural context affects model construction and validation.

Your reflection on inherent simplification is clear but remains implicit. Strengthen coherence by explicitly relating this insight to knowledge-framework elements like âassumptionsâ and âvalidity.â

Your analysis implicitly invokes knowledge-framework concepts. Make these explicit by naming âmethods,â âassumptions,â âscope,â and âvalidityâ when discussing each example to strengthen TOK integration.
Many examples remain at a descriptive level. Incorporate quantitative data or case studies to substantiate your claims, moving beyond assertion to evidence-based evaluation.
Overall structure is coherent with sustained focus on the title. The argument is logically sequenced, guiding the reader smoothly from introduction to conclusion.
Your final reflection captures the TOK theme of approximation. To leave a stronger impression, link this back to how knowledge claims in personal knowledge are influenced by perception and language.
Citation style remains inconsistent, mixing informal and formal references. Adopt a uniform academic style (e.g., APA or MLA) to enhance the essayâs scholarly rigour.
Evaluation of counterclaims is often brief. Allocate dedicated paragraphs to each counterclaim, developing them with specific evidence and linking back to your central thesis for balance.

reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
